summary.goal: Achieve sub-200ms launch-to-readable-content through careful optimization of the cold start path with measured phase budgets
summary.description:
Achieving sub-200ms launch-to-readable-content requires careful optimization of the cold start path.

## Cold Start Budget Breakdown (200ms total)

| Phase | Budget | What Happens |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| Process launch | ~30ms | dyld loads binary, system frameworks |
| AppDelegate init | ~10ms | Minimal setup, register for open-file |
| Receive file URL | ~5ms | macOS delivers open-file event |
| Start background parse | ~1ms | Dispatch cmark-gfm on background queue |
| Window + view creation | ~30ms | NSWindow, NSScrollView, NSTextView |
| Parse first chunk | ~20ms | Parse first ~10KB of markdown |
| Style + layout first screen | ~40ms | AttributedString + TextKit layout |
| First paint | ~15ms | Core Animation composites to screen |
| **Total first paint** | **~151ms** | Content visible to user |
| Background: parse remainder | continues | Remaining file parsed progressively |

## Optimization Techniques

### Binary Size
- No unnecessary frameworks linked
- Strip debug symbols in release
- Static linking preferred (avoids dyld overhead)
- Target: < 5MB binary

### App Lifecycle
- applicationWillFinishLaunching → register for file open events
- applicationDidFinishLaunching → minimal (no expensive setup)
- Do NOT eagerly load theme gallery, preferences, or non-essential UI
- Theme for current system appearance preloaded; other variant deferred

### Warm Launch (App Already Running)
- If app process is alive (dock icon, another window open), new file opens in ~50ms
- Only need: new window + parse + render
- No process startup overhead

### Pre-warming (Stretch Goal)
- macOS Launch Services can keep lightweight processes warm
- After quit, system may keep binary in disk cache
- Consider: leave minimal background process (< 2MB RSS) for near-instant relaunch
- Trade-off: user explicitly wants app to quit — this conflicts

## Measurement
- Instrument with os_signpost for each phase
- Profile with Instruments → App Launch template
- CI benchmark: fail build if launch exceeds 250ms on reference hardware
- Real-world test on oldest supported Mac hardware

acceptance_criteria.description: Stays within 200ms total launch-to-readable-content budget
Binary size under 5MB with minimal framework dependencies
Only essential components loaded at launch
os_signpost instrumentation for launch phase measurement

observations: Cold start budget: 151ms estimated for first paint (within 200ms target)
Static linking preferred to avoid dyld overhead
applicationWillFinishLaunching for file open events — minimal setup
Theme gallery and preferences lazily loaded
concerns: Pre-warming conflicts with user's 'quit on last window close' requirement
CI benchmarking on reference hardware needed

observations: Phase budget: process launch 30ms, window creation 30ms, parse 20ms, style+layout 40ms, paint 15ms
Profile with Instruments App Launch template

steps.title: Add os_signpost instrumentation to all launch phases
steps.description: Instrument the entire launch path with os_signpost for precise measurement. Add signposts at: (1) AppDelegate init start, (2) applicationWillFinishLaunching entry/exit, (3) applicationDidFinishLaunching entry/exit, (4) file URL received, (5) background parse dispatched, (6) window creation start/end, (7) first chunk parsed, (8) first chunk styled, (9) first layout complete, (10) first paint (via CATransaction.setCompletionBlock or similar). Use a dedicated OSLog subsystem (com.viewmd.launch) and category (performance).

acceptance_criteria.description: os_signpost intervals cover all 8 launch phases
Visible in Instruments App Launch template
Dedicated OSLog subsystem and category for launch performance
Total launch time measurable from signpost data

steps.title: Audit and minimize launch-path initialization
steps.description: Review all code that runs between process start and first paint. Move everything non-essential to lazy initialization. Specifically: (1) ThemeManager loads ONLY the current theme in the current system appearance at launch — other themes and the alternate appearance variant load lazily; (2) Settings/Preferences UI is NOT instantiated at launch; (3) No image cache pre-warming at launch; (4) TreeSitter grammars loaded lazily per-language on first use, not all at startup. Profile with Instruments to verify each phase stays within budget.

acceptance_criteria.description: Only current theme + current appearance loaded at launch
Settings UI not instantiated until first Cmd+, press
TreeSitter grammars loaded lazily per-language
Total launch-path init time under 20ms (excluding window and render)

steps.title: Optimize binary size and linking configuration
steps.description: Configure Xcode build settings to minimize binary size and dyld loading time. Enable: (1) Strip Swift symbols in release; (2) Dead code stripping; (3) Link Time Optimization (LTO) for release builds; (4) Static linking for SPM dependencies (cmark-gfm, TreeSitter); (5) Verify no unnecessary system frameworks linked (remove any auto-linked frameworks that are not used). Target binary size under 5MB.

acceptance_criteria.description: Release binary under 5MB
SPM dependencies statically linked
LTO enabled for release builds
Dead code stripping enabled
No unnecessary framework dependencies

steps.title: Implement warm-launch fast path
steps.description: Optimize the path when the app is already running and a new file is opened. In this case, process startup is skipped — only need: allocate window, start parse, render first screen. Target ~50ms. The PipelineCoordinator should have a fast path that reuses the already-loaded ThemeManager and TreeSitter instances. Window creation should be as lean as possible — reuse cached NSTextView configuration if feasible.

acceptance_criteria.description: New file opens in under 50ms when app is already running
ThemeManager and TreeSitter instances reused, not re-created
Window creation path is minimal (no redundant setup)
